message.content:
Review the following 5 files that implement a proximity-based docking system for "rocket mode" in a space-themed post viewer. Focus on:

1. Logic bugs (state machine transitions, race conditions, ref access safety)
2. Rendering bugs (canvas drawing order, NaN/Infinity guards)
3. Memory leaks (event listeners, intervals, rAF not cleaned up)
4. Edge cases (empty arrays, missing refs, division by zero)

Files to review:
- C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\Universe\hooks\useRocketMode.js
- C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\Universe\layers\RocketLayer.jsx
- C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\Universe\components\RocketHUD.jsx
- C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\Universe\Universe.jsx (focus on lines 563-700 and the handleRocketInteract / session restore logic)
- C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\Universe\Universe.module.css (just the new classes at the bottom)

The feature: When flying in "rocket mode", a user can fly toward stars (blog posts). As they get close (viewZ < 80), magnetic pull auto-aims toward the star. At viewZ < 18 and centered, auto-docking triggers with a 1.7s animation (approach → explosion → flash → navigate). Pressing ENTER within range also triggers docking. Session state is saved to sessionStorage so returning navigates back into rocket mode with a reverse warp effect.

Key state machine: 'none' → 'magnet' → 'approaching' → 'flash' → 'done'

Only report issues with HIGH confidence. Don't flag style preferences or minor nits.

content.text: I'll read all 5 files in parallel to begin the review.
